title: MCP HttpTransport missing timeout enables indefinite hangs
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/mcp.rs:284-293 (HttpTransport::new)
## Violation
The HTTP client is constructed with `reqwest::Client::new()`, which has no default timeout. Any MCP server that hangs (TCP open, no body) blocks the calling task forever. `StdioTransport`'s sibling code has no in-band timeout either; only `McpManager::call_tool_with_timeout` provides a tokio wrapper, but `call_tool` is exposed without one and so are `initialize`, `refresh_tools`, `list_resources`, `read_resource`.

```rust
pub fn new(base_url: &str) -> Self {
    Self {
        client: reqwest::Client::new(),
        ...
    }
}
```

Compare with `src/web.rs:265` which sets `.timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))`.
## Mandated refactor
Build the client with `Client::builder().timeout(Duration::from_secs(30)).connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).build()`. Apply a default per-request `tokio::time::timeout` inside every transport method (`StdioTransport::request`, `HttpTransport::request`) so the `McpError::Timeout` variant fires when a transport hangs.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
